title: Implement podman down command
body:
Add a "podman down kube" command analogous to "podman play kube".

Closes: #12475

Signed-off-by: Jonathan Yu <jawnsy@cpan.org>

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name.  Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **NOT APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *jawnsy* To complete the [pull request process](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process), please assign **umohnani8** after the PR has been reviewed. You can assign the PR to them by writing `/assign @umohnani8` in a comment when ready. The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman).
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - **[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)** Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Is there something wrong with `podman play kube --down foo.yaml`?  You didnt mention in your git commit message so i thought I make sure you were aware of it.
There's more discussion in the attached issue - the idea was to make this command isomorphic, like other commands like run/stop, push/pull

> There's more discussion in the attached issue - the idea was to make this command isomorphic, like other commands like run/stop, push/pull

Ok, cool @jawnsy . Given the debate in that issue, could you consider beefing up your commit message so it is clear what you decided?

couple of things to note:  you will now have to define and implement `down kube` in the RESTFUL API.  Depending on your approach, it might not be a lot of work at all.  But, you will definitely need to make the endpoint.  You will also need to create a golang restful binding.  And finally, you will need to make some tests both in e2e as well as maybe a system test and a binding test?
@jawnsy looking back, I listed all the things you still have to do!  But wanted to say on behalf of the team 'thank you' for being generous and making podman better.

> couple of things to note: you will now have to define and implement `down kube` in the RESTFUL API. Depending on your approach, it might not be a lot of work at all. But, you will definitely need to make the endpoint. You will also need to create a golang restful binding. And finally, you will need to make some tests both in e2e as well as maybe a system test and a binding test?

We already have a API endpoint why should we create another one? The cli does not have to match the api exactly.

Independent of this PR.  I think we should reflect on how to name the commands.  I would love to have a `podman kube down` instead of `down kube` (similar to the container, image and pod sub-commands).

`play kube` and `generate kube` could then be aliases for `kube play/generate`.
I like that UI a fair bit better, @vrothberg
I agree i also like podman kube down|up|generate
@jwhonce PTAL and particularily at the api stuff.

@jawnsy: PR needs rebase.

<details>

Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>
A friendly reminder that this PR had no activity for 30 days.
@jawnsy are you still working on this?

@rhatdan Thanks for the ping, and sorry that I haven't made progress on this in a little while. I started a new role last month, so haven't had a chance to look at this recently, but do still plan to get back to it. Is it okay if we leave this PR open, or would you prefer to close this out for now and I can open a new one when I'm ready?

@nicrowe00 started looking into https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/12475 which paves the road for a `down` command.  But as discussed in https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/12475, there will be no `down` command but a `kube down` command.  Things should be namespaced such as for `podman {containers,images,pods}`.  Hence, a `podman kube {play,down,...}`.
